text.title: Cognitive Inclusion Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the cognitive-inclusion-design methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Flows with >3 sequential steps MUST be chunked with explicit progress indicators showing position + remaining steps.
rule.rationale: Working memory degrades with step count; chunking + progress restores predictability.

rule.statement: Interaction outcomes MUST be predictable: button labels state the action (`Submit application`), not the location (`Next`); navigation order is stable; destructive actions confirm.
rule.rationale: Unpredictable outcomes amplify anxiety and force re-checking; predictability reduces cognitive load.

rule.statement: Every multi-step flow MUST expose a 'Save and exit' escape; abandonment without save is forbidden.
rule.rationale: ADHD + anxiety users abandon long flows; lost-work fear discourages return.

rule.statement: Reduced-motion + extended-time settings MUST be exposed in product settings; the system MUST respect OS-level prefers-reduced-motion.
rule.rationale: Vestibular + cognitive users need control; ignoring OS-level prefers-reduced-motion is SC 2.3.3 + 2.2.1 risk.

rule.statement: Surfaces with reading-level above Grade 8 (Flesch-Kincaid) MUST expose a plain-language toggle with Grade 5-6 alternative.
rule.rationale: Low-literacy + non-native speakers need simplified text; offering toggle preserves expert content.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
